It tells the agent exactly WHAT to change (e.g., 'Navigate to /per-page-config/hub-a, hub-b, hub-c instead of using await browser.back()'), which hub pages to create, what imports to use, and what documentation to write. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Most fail-to-pass tests are pure text/file content checks: test_hub_pages_structure checks for string presence ('LinkAccordion', 'await connection()', '<Suspense', 'export default function Page()'), test_stale_pages_link_to_hubs checks string presence, test_test_uses_hub_navigation checks string presence, test_agents_md_router_act_rule checks string presence, test_router_act_skill_created checks string presence. None of them actually compile, run, or execute the code. The pass-to-pass tests run

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply creates files with the right string patterns would pass all fail-to-pass tests. For example, test_hub_pages_structure only checks for string inclusion of 'LinkAccordion', 'await connection()', '<Suspense', 'export default function Page()' — a file containing all these strings as comments would pass. No test actually compiles or runs the Next.js pages.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution exists at /solution/solve.sh in the container and could be read directly by the agent. The tests only check for file existence and string patterns, so the agent could also grep the test file (test_outputs.py) to extract exact expected strings. There are no measures preventing these approaches.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors listed in instruction.md (hub pages structure, stale page connectivity, test navigation assertions, AGENTS.md documentation, SKILL.md creation) are tested by corresponding test functions.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are clearly grouped with section comments (Pass-to-pass static, Pass-to-pass repo_tests, Fail-to-pass pr_diff code behavior, Fail-to-pass config/instruction), well-named functions, and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task produces code files and documentation, not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, commands, and variable names in the instruction and tests are consistent and correctly spelled.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact test ('per-page value overrides global staleTimes.dynamic regardless of direction'), specifies exactly which calls to remove ('browser.back()'), what to replace them with (hub page navigation to hub-a/b/c), and the exact file structure to create. This goes beyond identifying the buggy area into prescribing the exact fix.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is primarily a code task with documentation additions, not a config-edit-only task.
